message:
fix(web/sse): normalize bare CR in comment lines to block field injection

Found via fresh-eyes audit. The data field already normalized bare \r to
\n (matching the WHATWG SSE spec) before splitting into lines so a payload
like "a\rdata: injected" could not produce a real `data:` field on the
wire, but the comment field used Rust's `str::lines()` which splits on
\n and \r\n but NOT bare \r. A comment such as "safe\rdata: injected"
would emit ":safe\rdata: injected\n" verbatim, and the EventSource
parser would treat the bare \r as a line break and interpret the second
half as a real field.

Apply the same \r\n→\n then \r→\n normalization the data field already
uses, then split with `lines()` so the existing trailing-newline behavior
is preserved. Adds two regression tests covering bare-\r injection and
the standard \r\n round-trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
